shift: Find cap near shifter base. Remove.
neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.notes: Manual: clutch to N. RWD. High-perf models have strong parking pawl — apply firm pressure on slot.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front (rear/drive wheels roll on ground) or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from FRONT. Rear (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If front-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the FRONT wheels and tow rear-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

knownIssues.title: Strong Parking Pawl on High-Performance Models
knownIssues.detail: Hellcat, Redeye, and Demon models have an exceptionally strong parking pawl that requires significant force on the manual override slot. Press firmly and hold for 3-5 seconds before attempting to move the shifter. Operators sometimes give up too early thinking the slot isn't working.

battery.warnings: Hellcat/Redeye/Demon: parking pawl is very strong — press slot firmly for 3-5 seconds.

shift: Find cap near shifter base. Remove.
neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from front — rear (drive) wheels roll on ground. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from FRONT. Rear (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If front-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the FRONT wheels and tow rear-first.
AWD VARIANT: Use flatbed. If no flatbed, wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: WITH POWER: Press brake. Select N.
WITHOUT POWER: Find slot near selector base. Insert key, press down. Press brake. Select N.
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

battery.warnings: NEVER touch HV battery. Flatbed only.

epb.override: Restore 12V. Hold EPB button to release.
steps: Hold EPB button until brake releases.

vehicles.steeringLock: Electronic steering lock (push-button start models). Will engage without ignition — use flatbed or ensure steering is not locked during tow.

vehicles.slot: Cap at console shifter (older) or slot near electronic selector (newer)
shift: Find cap/slot near shifter or selector. Remove cap if present.
neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from front — rear (drive) wheels roll on ground. AWD/4WD: transfer case to 2WD or flatbed. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric push-button (NV244 or NV140 depending on year)
positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD — NOT safe for towing
4WD Hi — part-time 4WD — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Press the 2WD button on the transfer case control.
Safe to lift front (drive) wheels once confirmed in 2WD.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.

shift: Find cap near shifter base. Remove.
neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear (front/drive wheels roll on ground) or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

shift: Find cap near shifter base. Remove.
neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear — front (drive) wheels roll on ground. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter base
shift: Find cap at side of console shifter. Remove.
neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.notes: 4WD: transfer case. Manual: clutch to N.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from front — rear (drive) wheels roll on ground. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Manual floor lever or electric push-button depending on trim
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: MANUAL: Stop completely. Shift TC floor lever to 2H.
ELECTRIC: Slow under 3 MPH. Press 2H. Confirm indicator.
Safe to lift rear wheels in 2H.
NEVER tow in 4H on dry pavement or in 4L.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Manual: no shift lock. Auto: slot near selector
shift: MANUAL (most Vipers): No shift lock — depress clutch, select Neutral.
AUTO (rare): Find slot near selector. Insert key, press down. Press brake. Move to N.
neutral: Auto: connect jump pack, shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.notes: Most Vipers are manual. SRT/ACR: manual only.
vehicles.warn: EXTREMELY LOW CLEARANCE — flatbed ONLY. Side lift recommended.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — extremely low clearance, side lift recommended
towNotes: RWD: Lift from FRONT. Rear (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If front-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the FRONT wheels and tow rear-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

knownIssues.title: Extremely Low Ground Clearance — Side Lift Required
knownIssues.detail: The Viper has approximately 3.7 inches of ground clearance. Standard flatbed ramps cannot be used without damaging the front splitter and underbody. Side-lift equipment or very shallow approach ramps with extensions are required. Never winch from the front.

knownIssues.title: Most Vipers Are Manual Transmission
knownIssues.detail: The vast majority of Vipers have manual transmissions — no shift lock, no neutral override needed. Simply depress the clutch and select neutral. Automatic Viper variants are extremely rare. Verify before spending time on neutral override procedures.

battery.location: Under hood, passenger side near firewall
battery.warnings: Side-lift or special ramps only. Carbon ceramic brakes on some trims — no metal chocks against rotors.
